messages.content: Find where the runtime tracks fallback activation for the bridge.
src/tok/gateway.py: logger.warning("tok_fallback_activated: session degraded to baseline after %d consecutive fallback events", ...)
Open the relevant implementation so I can see the session behavior too.
Same question, but phrased differently: where does Tok mark a session as baseline-only after repeated failures?

content.text: The baseline-only switch is in RuntimeSession.record_fallback_event in src/tok/universal_runtime.py. It increments the consecutive fallback counter, flips _baseline_only to True at the threshold, and emits the tok_fallback_activated warning.

messages.content: And if I asked about the bridge fallback path again, what file would you anchor on?

content.text: I would anchor on src/tok/gateway.py for the bridge path and src/tok/universal_runtime.py for the RuntimeSession fallback state logic.

messages.content: Great, summarize it one last time in plain language.

content.text: Tok logs the bridge fallback event in src/tok/gateway.py, but the session actually becomes baseline-only inside RuntimeSession.record_fallback_event in src/tok/universal_runtime.py after repeated fallback events.
